takes.event_basis: <cite index="15-28">NVIDIA scheduled to report earnings for first quarter of fiscal 2027 on May 20</cite> after market close.
takes.thesis: <cite index="12-7,12-10">Major hyperscalers raised FY2026 CAPEX by ~$51 billion (+77% YoY), signaling stronger demand for NVDA's AI accelerators</cite>. <cite index="14-27">CEO Jensen Huang expects at least $1 trillion of opportunity tied to Blackwell and Rubin systems through 2027</cite>. <cite index="15-32,15-34">Prediction markets overwhelmingly favor Nvidia beating expectations for Q1, with implied probability around 90% on Polymarket</cite>.
takes.risks: Stock already at all-time highs; margin pressure from competing AI chips (AMD, custom accelerators) and rising memory costs could compress guidance

takes.event_basis: Q1 FY27 Earnings on 2026-05-22 (after market close). CEO Huang's participation in US-China summit signals potential export pathway recovery; hyperscaler capex spending on track for $725B+ combined annually.
takes.thesis: <cite index="31-8">CEO Huang's participation in US delegation summit with China suggests potential pathway for advanced chip exports, removing regulatory headwinds on Chinese demand.</cite> Combined with sustained AI capex momentum and <cite index="6-9">hyperscaler combined 2026 spending projections tracking near $725 billion, above earlier expectations,</cite> NVIDIA positioned for earnings beat and raised guidance.
takes.risks: Geopolitical detente could reverse if US-Iran tensions escalate; inflation surprise may prompt Warsh Fed to signal rate-hold policy extending into H2 2026.

takes.event_basis: Intel diversification deal validation; path to WWDC (June 4) with expected AI announcements. <cite index="47-4">Apple hit all-time intraday high of $290.33</cite>, now at $300.
takes.thesis: <cite index="32-5">Deal would gain steady stream of demand for Intel foundry, key milestone for contract manufacturing</cite>. WWDC catalyst sets up product strategy for 2026-27 iPhone/Mac cycle with manufacturing optionality. Supply chain diversification reduces TSMC concentration risk.
takes.risks: Stock at all-time highs with sentiment elevated; any product announcement shortfall or delayed Intel ramp could trigger profit-taking.

takes.event_basis: <cite index="10-1,10-6">WWDC2026 online June 8-12 with Keynote and Platforms State of the Union on Monday, June 8</cite>.
takes.thesis: <cite index="10-4">WWDC26 will spotlight incredible updates for Apple platforms, including AI advancements and exciting new software and developer tools</cite>. <cite index="1-7">iPhone achieved March quarter revenue record, fueled by iPhone 17 lineup</cite>. Leadership transition to Ternus (September 1) signals operational continuity while Cook can focus on geopolitical/government relations.
takes.risks: Foldable iPhone investor expectations may exceed reality; iPhone 18 demand in fall could disappoint if AI features lack differentiation vs. Android competitors

takes.event_basis: <cite index="27-1">Firm customer commitments expected to begin in second half of 2026</cite>; Apple M7/A21 deal preliminary but validation of 18A-P process credibility.
takes.thesis: <cite index="22-4,22-5,22-15">Apple and Intel signed preliminary chipmaking agreement in December 2025; if the rumor is accurate, Intel could begin making Apple's M7 by 2027 and an iPhone class A21 chip by 2028</cite>. <cite index="28-2">CFO David Zinsner noted company continues to improve yields on its older nodes, such as Intel 4 and Intel 3, while refining yield of the current top-performing 18A node</cite>.
takes.risks: 18A yields may not meet Apple's stringent quality bar; loss-making foundry division adds execution risk; competitive pressure from TSMC and Samsung remains
